perturbation.original_text: 1.1. "Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient" or "API" shall have the meaning given such term in the preamble hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. "Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient" or "API" shall be determined by INTERSECT.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'API' from the preamble to a determination by INTERSECT introduces ambiguity. The preamble defines API with reference to Exhibit A, providing a clear and objective standard. The changed definition gives INTERSECT unilateral control, potentially allowing them to alter the API's definition without HOVIONE's consent or any objective criteria. This contradicts Section 5.1, which requires HOVIONE to manufacture API according to specifications and applicable laws since the term in the definition is now determined by INTERSECT.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1. Manufacture. The API shall be manufactured by HOVIONE at its facilities in accordance with all relevant current Good Manufacturing Practices ("cGMPs"), the Specifications, and Applicable Laws, and pursuant to HOVIONE's Drug Master File ("DMF"), prepared by HOVIONE and filed with the US FDA. HOVIONE shall advise INTERSECT in writing in advance of making any changes to the Product Specifications or any material changes in the methods, processes or procedures in manufacturing the API that could affect the quality, purity and/or physical properties of the API, any changes will be made according to the terms of the quality agreement between the parties. HOVIONE shall provide sufficient notice of any such change to INTERSECT to allow INTERSECT to make any required notices to and obtain any required approvals from any Regulatory Agency with respect to such change.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'API' from a specific, objective standard (reference to the preamble) to a determination solely by INTERSECT. This directly contradicts Section 5.1, which requires HOVIONE to manufacture API according to 'the Specifications'. If INTERSECT unilaterally determines the 'API', it undermines HOVIONE's obligation and introduces ambiguity about the specifications HOVIONE must follow, creating a clear conflict and ambiguity. This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict in the document regarding API specifications.

perturbation.original_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within thirty (30) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT determines that a lot of API does not conform to the Product Specifications, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE. Any API rejected by INTERSECT may not be reshipped to INTERSECT except if the API is reprocessed according to the DMF. HOVIONE sole responsibility shall be to replace any non-conforming API within thirty (30) days of receiving the notice of non- conformity. Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by a mutually acceptable third-party testing laboratory located in a neutral country. HOVIONE shall pay all the fees of the third-party laboratory, unless the third-party testing laboratory determines that the delivered API materially conforms to the Product Specifications, in which case INTERSECT shall pay all the fees of such third-party laboratory and also any additional costs that HOVIONE incurred in providing replacement material.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within thirty (30) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT determines that a lot of API does not conform to the Product Specifications, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE. Any API rejected by INTERSECT may not be reshipped to INTERSECT. Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by internal INTERSECT audit.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause about third-party resolution of disputes and reprocessed API and replacing it with 'internal INTERSECT audit' introduces a conflict of interest and an in-text contradiction.  The original text establishes a mechanism for impartial assessment. The modified text gives INTERSECT sole authority to resolve disputes, potentially biasing the outcome in their favor. This change conflicts with the implied fairness and objectivity of product specification adherence and it is in conflict with Section 5.1 which talks about HOVIONE being advised in writing on change of product specifications which could affect the quality. With the change, INTERSECT internal audit is the only thing that matters and it could affect the quality of the API and HOVIONE wouldn't be advised.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1. Manufacture. The API shall be manufactured by HOVIONE at its facilities in accordance with all relevant current Good Manufacturing Practices ("cGMPs"), the Specifications, and Applicable Laws, and pursuant to HOVIONE's Drug Master File ("DMF"), prepared by HOVIONE and filed with the US FDA. HOVIONE shall advise INTERSECT in writing in advance of making any changes to the Product Specifications or any material changes in the methods, processes or procedures in manufacturing the API that could affect the quality, purity and/or physical properties of the API, any changes will be made according to the terms of the quality agreement between the parties. HOVIONE shall provide sufficient notice of any such change to INTERSECT to allow INTERSECT to make any required notices to and obtain any required approvals from any Regulatory Agency with respect to such change.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 5.6 replaces the third-party dispute resolution mechanism with an internal INTERSECT audit. This directly contradicts Section 5.1, which states that HOVIONE must be advised in writing of any changes to product specifications or manufacturing processes that could affect API quality. By removing the independent third-party assessment, the modified Section 5.6 allows INTERSECT to potentially accept non-conforming API without HOVIONE's knowledge or input, directly conflicting with HOVIONE's right to be informed of changes affecting API quality as outlined in Section 5.1. The original text ensured a more impartial assessment, while the changed text introduces a conflict of interest.

perturbation.original_text: 14.1. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any interest herein may be assigned, in whole or in part, by either party without the prior written consent of the other, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed, except that either party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement: (a) to an affiliate, division or subsidiary of such party; and/or (b) to any third party that acquires all or substantially all of the stock or assets of such party, whether by asset sale, stock sale, merger or otherwise, and, in any such event such assignee shall assume the transferring party's obligations hereunder. However, notwithstanding any such assignment, in the case of an assignment to an affiliate, division or subsidiary, the transferring party shall remain liable under this Agreement (in addition to the transferee) unless such liability is specifically waived in writing by the other party hereto. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto, and their respective successors and permitted assigns. (a) Buyout. In the case that either company is acquired by, or merges with, another company which has reason to not wish to continue the relationship, that company may make a contract buyout payment [*] for the [*], with a [*] buyout payment amount of [*].
perturbation.changed_text: 14.1. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any interest herein may be assigned, in whole or in part, by either party without the prior written consent of the other, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed, except that either party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement: (a) to an affiliate, division or subsidiary of such party; and/or (b) to any third party that acquires all or substantially all of the stock or assets of such party, whether by asset sale, stock sale, merger or otherwise, and, in any such event such assignee shall assume the transferring party's obligations hereunder. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto, and their respective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'Buyout' clause introduces a significant in-text contradiction. The clause provides a mechanism for a buyout payment if a company is acquired and the acquiring company doesn't want to continue the agreement. By removing this clause, Section 14.1, which initially allowed assignment under specific conditions including acquisition, now lacks a clear exit strategy for acquisitions where the acquiring entity doesn't want to continue the agreement. This omission creates ambiguity because while assignment is allowed, there's no provision to terminate the agreement if the acquiring company chooses to stop operations. The acquiring company will have to buy API per 2.1 Supply which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Supply. During the term of this Agreement and subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, INTERSECT shall purchase [*] of its annual API requirement, from HOVIONE and HOVIONE shall manufacture and supply API to INTERSECT (or a third party designated by INTERSECT) in such quantities as from time to time may be ordered by INTERSECT.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the buyout clause from Section 14.1 creates a contradiction with Section 2.1. Section 14.1 originally allowed for assignment of the agreement, including in cases of acquisition, and the buyout clause provided a mechanism for termination if the acquiring company did not wish to continue the agreement. Now, without the buyout clause, Section 2.1, which mandates that INTERSECT purchase its annual API requirement from HOVIONE, remains in effect even if INTERSECT is acquired by a company that does not want to continue purchasing API from HOVIONE. This creates a direct conflict, as the acquiring company is obligated to purchase API per 2.1, despite the changed 14.1.
